title: 1.1: States that Kitty's own process exits with status code 0 when the child program exits with status 0.
1.2: States the completion message format containing text elements such as 'Command', 'finished with status', and 'Click to focus'
1.5: States that SIGCHLD is the OS-level signal Kitty listens for to detect child process termination
1.6: States that waitpid() is the system call Kitty uses to retrieve the child's exit status
1.7: States that OSC 133 escape sequences are the protocol mechanism that carries exit status from shell to the message-generating function
1.8: States that child program output appears in Kitty's terminal window (via PTY) rather than the outer shell
2.1: Claims that the completion message is always displayed automatically without any configuration requirements
2.2: Claims that shell integration features work without any shell-side setup or configuration
